principle.name: Navigation Hierarchy Clarity
principle.description: Documentation navigation must be intuitive and self-explanatory. Users should never feel lost or wonder where they are in the documentation structure.
principle.rationale: Clear navigation reduces frustration and increases productivity. Professional docs like Appwrite and Tailwind excel at making complex structures feel simple.

example.good: Categories: Authentication, Database, Storage, Functions, Teams — each with clear icons and expandable subsections
example.bad: Flat list of 50+ links with no grouping or visual hierarchy

principle.name: Content Discoverability
principle.description: Every piece of content should be findable within 3 clicks. Use consistent linking patterns and cross-reference related topics.
principle.rationale: Users often arrive via search directly to specific pages. They need clear paths to discover related content.

example.scenario: When a user lands on an API endpoint page
example.good: Show related endpoints, common use cases, error references, and "next topic" navigation
example.bad: Isolated page with no connections to broader context

principle.name: Code Example Quality
principle.description: All code examples must be copy-paste ready, well-commented, and include usage context. Examples should work without modification.
principle.rationale: Developers use documentation primarily for code examples. Poor examples waste user time and erode trust.

example.good: Complete working example with import statements, error handling, and comments explaining each step
example.bad: Snippets that require users to fill in placeholder variables

principle.name: Visual Consistency
principle.description: Maintain consistent typography, spacing, and visual patterns across all documentation pages.
principle.rationale: Visual consistency reduces cognitive load and makes content easier to scan and digest.

example.good: Syntax highlighting with consistent theme, line numbers for long examples, copy button in same position
example.bad: Inconsistent code block styles across different pages

principle.name: Search as First-Class Feature
principle.description: Search must understand natural language queries and documentation structure. Results should include context and citations.
principle.rationale: Professional documentation platforms like Algolia-powered docs show that search quality directly impacts user satisfaction.

example.scenario: User queries "how do I configure authentication"
example.good: Returns configuration steps, code examples, related options, and links with proper section citations
example.bad: Returns only exact keyword matches with no context

convention.rule: File and directory names should be lowercase with hyphens
Documentation directory structure should separate content types
convention.template: docs/ getting-started/ guides/ api-reference/ tutorials/ components/

convention.rule: All markdown files should include YAML frontmatter
convention.template: --- title: Page Title description: Brief description for SEO and search category: Category Name tags: [tag1, tag2] order: 1 ---

pitfall.why_problematic: Deep nesting and complex hierarchies make content hard to find
pitfall.correct_approach: Follow the "three click rule" — any content should be reachable within 3 navigation actions

pitfall.why_problematic: Mixed formatting, missing imports, or non-working examples erode user trust
pitfall.correct_approach: Create a code example template and enforce linting for documentation code blocks

pitfall.why_problematic: Many users access documentation from mobile devices; broken mobile experience reflects poorly on the product
pitfall.correct_approach: Design mobile-first with collapsible sidebars and touch-friendly interactions

pitfall.why_problematic: Search results that don't understand documentation structure return irrelevant matches
pitfall.correct_approach: Build search that indexes content structure (headings, code blocks, examples) separately for targeted results

category.name: before_starting
category.item: Understand the target audience and their primary use cases
Define the documentation hierarchy and content categories
Review existing documentation for consistency patterns

category.name: during_implementation
category.item: Follow consistent naming and structure conventions
Include proper frontmatter for all markdown files
Test code examples in clean environments

category.name: before_completion
category.item: Test search with natural language queries
Validate responsive design on mobile viewports
